items.title: 2 Button Harbinger Power 32k
items.rotation:
Rotation is dagger 2, shroud, shroud 2. Make sure to cast dagger 2 right before and right after you enter/exit shroud.

Precast elite skill and/or heal skill for blight stacks. You can run golem instead if you need CC. However recasting heal/elite can be helpful if you lose blight during boss phases for example.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity necromancer build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 Rotation
2:27 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
So, for the rundown, I hope the build is extremely self-explanatory, but all you really want to do is camp Harbinger Shroud, and your goal outside of Harbinger Shroud is to get back into Harbinger Shroud. The only two rules are that you want to make sure that you Life Siphon right before you enter Harbinger Shroud and you Life Siphon right after you exit Harbinger Shroud. You can throw in two wells. That's the four-button build. In case you're seeing this on the two-button build, I would recommend checking that out. It's really, really easy. It's a decent DPS increase for pretty much no effort.

Notable skills: Warhorn 4 is a CC. That's the reason why I run warhorn offhand on this build, so make sure you press four whenever you see a break bar. In Harbinger Shroud, skills 4 and 5 are CC skills, so make sure you press those whenever there's a break bar.

The only thing I want to mention is that your heal skill and elite skill are kind of free. You can run whatever you want. You can keep the heal skill as is and precast it in fights to start off with some blight. But your elite skill, I would probably recommend running Flesh Golem on most fights that have a break bar. This is going to be way more valuable than whatever small DPS increase you get from precasting the elite elixir.

For useful utility skills, Necromancer has a few really strong ones. Corrosive Poison Cloud is a projectile destruction field that you put down on the ground, so basically any projectiles that try to fly into the circle are going to be deleted. Epidemic is not so great on a power build, but it can be useful in instances where you're just looking to get some basic conditions like chill or weakness on an enemy.

Spectral Grasp gives you life force, gives you CC, and is a really long-range pull, so that's something to consider. Elixir of Risk is also good if you need extra condi cleanse. I hope you all enjoy the build. I hope you all see success with it. Thank you.

items.title: 4 Button Harbinger Power 34.6k
items.rotation:
Opener is both wells into dagger 2 into shroud into shroud 2 spam.

Rotation is basically dagger 2 when you exit shroud, both wells, auto attack until dagger 2 comes up, cast dagger 2 before you enter shroud, spam shroud 2. Repeat.

Precast elite skill and/or heal skill for blight stacks. You can run golem instead if you need CC. However recasting heal/elite can be helpful if you lose blight during boss phases for example.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity necromancer build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 Rotation
2:16 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
So, for the rundown, I hope that the build is extremely self-explanatory, but all you really want to do is camp Harbinger Shroud. Your goal outside of Harbinger Shroud is to get back into Harbinger Shroud. The only two rules are that you want to make sure that you Life Siphon right before you enter Harbinger Shroud and you Life Siphon right after you exit Harbinger Shroud. You can throw in two wells. That's the four-button build. In case you're seeing this on the two-button build, I would recommend checking that out. It's really, really easy. It's a pretty decent DPS increase for pretty much no effort.

Notable skills: Warhorn 4 is a CC. That's the reason why I run Warhorn offhand on this build, so make sure you press four whenever you see a break bar. In Harbinger Shroud, skills 4 and 5 are CC skills, so make sure you press those whenever there's a break bar.

The only thing I want to mention is that your heal skill and elite skill are kind of free. You can run whatever you want. You can keep the heal skill as is and precast it in fights to start off with some blight. But your elite skill, I would probably recommend running Flesh Golem on most fights that have a break bar. This is going to be way more valuable than whatever small DPS increase you get from precasting the elite elixir.

For useful utility skills, Necromancer has a few really strong ones. Corrosive Poison Cloud is a projectile destruction field that you put down on the ground, so basically any projectiles that try to fly into the circle are going to be deleted. Epidemic is not so great on a power build, but can be useful in instances where you're just looking to get some basic conditions, maybe chill or immobilize, on an enemy. A few places I can think of are maybe Deimos CM, where you Epidemic off of Deimos to stop the prides from coming in, or maybe Soulless Horror in Wing 5. Spectral Grasp gives you life force, gives you CC, and is a really long-range pull. Well of Power is also good if you need extra condition cleanse. I hope you all enjoy the build and I hope you all see success with it. Thank you.

items.title: 4 Button Harbinger Condi 32k Parasitic Contagion
items.rotation:
Opener is heal skill into elite into shroud into shroud 2 spam.

Rotation is pistol 2 into focus 4 into elite (if available) into focus 4 into pistol 2 into maybe focus 4 if it's up into shroud and shroud 2 spam. Repeat.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity necromancer build for fractal/raid/strike content.

I had a lot of problems with necromancer surprisingly. I think this is maybe still a little undercooked and I may come back to this. I think necromancer is generally "faceroll" where you want to cast all of your skills so it's kind of hard to "condense" that into a few clicks.

Basically the problem is that 2 buttons are always shroud, shroud 2 (and weapon skill 2). Either condition elite is better than BiP from my testing so that's the 3rd button. So 4th button has to be life force generation I think... hence why I ran parasitic contagion. We can't fit in corruptions well. Life force generation was lacking hard so I ran focus which I think is best for life force regen in this scenario.

Dagger main hand ended up being low damage. I think the lack of conditions during non shroud periods loses hard. Basically dagger had a damage problem rather than a life force problem and it was hard to fix so I gave up on it. I try to aim for my builds to be 30k+ dps.

Let me know if you have any ideas for improving it! I may come back to it later. At least the nice thing is that with Parasitic Contagion you will be very healthy.
